{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Corporate Sales Executive",
  "description": "LE TREDA Consult - Our client was incorporated in 2001. The company works in ICT integration and connectivity, with a product range that spans quick-to-deploy radios and space segment allocation.

They are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Corporate Sales Executive
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;

Job Responsibilities


	Identify and develop new B2B business opportunities, promoting products and services to new and existing clients.
	Manage existing client relationships, understand their needs, and deliver a high standard of service.
	Approach prospective businesses with tailored proposals, including concept selling where a client&#39;s need isn&#39;t yet defined as a specific product.
	Maintain the sales and client database.
	Generate new business within the assigned territory or channel.
	Prepare proposals for target clients, including relevant technical information and quotes.
	Manage client and project receivables and cash collection against financial KPIs.
	Communicate with clients before and after each deal to ensure a positive experience throughout.
	Work closely with Finance, Customer Support,&nbsp;&nbsp;Engineering and CRM to keep service delivery and after-sales support running smoothly.
	Act as a team player, working proactively with management and colleagues to improve overall company performance.
	Build long-term relationships with clients in the target channel or market, managing expectations against company guidelines.


Person Specification


	Degree educated.
	Minimum 5 years of experience in a similar corporate sales role.
	Background in IT or insurance policy sales preferred.
	Experience with concept selling &mdash; comfortable selling a solution before a client has defined their own requirement.
	Team player with leadership capability.
	Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to build new relationships.
	Strong verbal and written communication.
	Confident presenter.
	Reliable and works with integrity.


Salary
N100,000 - N500,000 per month.&nbsp;&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: letreda@gmail.com using &ldquo;Corporate Sales Executive&rdquo; as the subject bof the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-08-16",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Le Treda"
    
  },

Corporate Sales Executive Job at LE TREDA Consult




        
    

        
        LE TREDA Consult - Our client was incorporated in 2001. The company works in ICT integration and connectivity, with a product range that spans quick-to-deploy radios and space segment allocation.

They are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Corporate Sales Executive
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time 

Job Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Identify and develop new B2B business opportunities, promoting products and services to new and existing clients.
	Manage existing client relationships, understand their needs, and deliver a high standard of service.
	Approach prospective businesses with tailored proposals, including concept selling where a client's need isn't yet defined as a specific product.
	Maintain the sales and client database.
	Generate new business within the assigned territory or channel.
	Prepare proposals for target clients, including relevant technical information and quotes.
	Manage client and project receivables and cash collection against financial KPIs.
	Communicate with clients before and after each deal to ensure a positive experience throughout.
	Work closely with Finance, Customer Support,  Engineering and CRM to keep service delivery and after-sales support running smoothly.
	Act as a team player, working proactively with management and colleagues to improve overall company performance.
	Build long-term relationships with clients in the target channel or market, managing expectations against company guidelines.


Person Specification


	Degree educated.
	Minimum 5 years of experience in a similar corporate sales role.
	Background in IT or insurance policy sales preferred.
	Experience with concept selling — comfortable selling a solution before a client has defined their own requirement.
	Team player with leadership capability.
	Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to build new relationships.
	Strong verbal and written communication.
	Confident presenter.
	Reliable and works with integrity.


Salary
N100,000 - N500,000 per month.  

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Corporate Sales Executive” as the subject bof the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Executive",
  "description": "Montaigne Place is a leading luxury retail company that offers an exquisite range of cosmetics, fragrances, skincare, lifestyle products, spa and wellness services. Established in 1995, Montaigne Place is a trading name of Montaigne AH. Ltd. began its journey in the Nigerian beauty sector as exclusive agents and distributors of the prestigious Clarins brand.

Montaigne Place is renowned for curating an impressive collection of sought-after brands in fragrances, skincare, and cosmetics. Its exclusive portfolio comprises more than a dozen top-tier names, including Creed, Roja Parfum, Ajmal, The Spirit of Dubai, Parfums de Marly, Atelier des Ors, Yves Rocher, Murad and more. In addition to these exclusive brands, the company also stocks mainstream favorites such as Clarins, Chanel, YSL, Armani, Bulgari, Lancome and others.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos

Responsibilities


	Identify and generate new business opportunities through lead generation, cold calling, networking, and market research.
	Conduct product or service presentations and demonstrations tailored to client needs
	Negotiate contracts, finalize deals, and ensure customer satisfaction.
	Maintain and strengthen relationships with existing clients, providing post-sales support and addressing feedback
	Prepare sales reports, monitor market trends, and track competitor activity
	Forecast sales, set performance targets, and consistently meet or exceed quotas
	Represent the company at trade shows, conferences, and industry events to generate leads and enhance brand visibility
	Collaborate with marketing, customer support, and other internal teams to ensure a seamless client experience.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or a related field.
	Proven experience in sales, preferably in B2B or relevant industry sectors.
	Strong interpersonal and communication skills to engage effectively with clients
	Negotiation, presentation, and closing skills to achieve sales goals.
	Proficiency in CRM software (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot) and MS Office.
	Time management, problem-solving, and business acumen to handle multiple clients and market dynamics.
	Adaptability, self-motivation, and a results-driven approach.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hephzibahcareers1@gmail.com using the Job Position as subject of the email.",

Sales Executive Job at Montaigne Place




        
    

        
        Montaigne Place is a leading luxury retail company that offers an exquisite range of cosmetics, fragrances, skincare, lifestyle products, spa and wellness services. Established in 1995, Montaigne Place is a trading name of Montaigne AH. Ltd. began its journey in the Nigerian beauty sector as exclusive agents and distributors of the prestigious Clarins brand.
     

            
    



Montaigne Place is renowned for curating an impressive collection of sought-after brands in fragrances, skincare, and cosmetics. Its exclusive portfolio comprises more than a dozen top-tier names, including Creed, Roja Parfum, Ajmal, The Spirit of Dubai, Parfums de Marly, Atelier des Ors, Yves Rocher, Murad and more. In addition to these exclusive brands, the company also stocks mainstream favorites such as Clarins, Chanel, YSL, Armani, Bulgari, Lancome and others.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Executive

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos

Responsibilities


	Identify and generate new business opportunities through lead generation, cold calling, networking, and market research.
	Conduct product or service presentations and demonstrations tailored to client needs
	Negotiate contracts, finalize deals, and ensure customer satisfaction.
	Maintain and strengthen relationships with existing clients, providing post-sales support and addressing feedback
	Prepare sales reports, monitor market trends, and track competitor activity
	Forecast sales, set performance targets, and consistently meet or exceed quotas
	Represent the company at trade shows, conferences, and industry events to generate leads and enhance brand visibility
	Collaborate with marketing, customer support, and other internal teams to ensure a seamless client experience.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, or a related field.
	Proven experience in sales, preferably in B2B or relevant industry sectors.
	Strong interpersonal and communication skills to engage effectively with clients
	Negotiation, presentation, and closing skills to achieve sales goals.
	Proficiency in CRM software (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot) and MS Office.
	Time management, problem-solving, and business acumen to handle multiple clients and market dynamics.
	Adaptability, self-motivation, and a results-driven approach.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Accor Hotel is a French multinational hospitality company that owns, manages, and franchises hotels, resorts, and vacation properties. It is the largest hospitality company in Europe and the sixth-largest hospitality company worldwide.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Finance Manager
Job Location: Lagos

Description


	We are looking for a dynamic and experienced Finance Manager to lead the financial operations of the hotel and support strategic decision-making that drives business performance and profitability.
	Reporting to the General Manager, the Finance Manager will oversee financial reporting, budgeting, compliance, and internal controls while ensuring alignment with the financial standards of Accor.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the overall financial operations of the hotel including accounting, reporting, and financial controls
	Prepare and analyze monthly financial reports, forecasts, and annual budgets
	Ensure compliance with Accor financial policies, internal audit standards, and statutory regulations
	Monitor cash flow, cost control, and revenue performance to support operational profitability
	Supervise and develop the finance team while ensuring adherence to financial procedures
	Coordinate internal and external audits and ensure timely resolution of audit findings
	Provide financial insights and strategic recommendations to support management decision-making
	Ensure timely tax filings, statutory payments, and regulatory compliance


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field
	5&ndash;10 years of relevant&nbsp;work experience, preferably in hospitality, service, or corporate environments
	ICAN, ACCA, or an internationally recognized equivalent professional certification
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting, budgeting, cost control, and internal audit processes
	Proficiency in accounting systems and financial management tools
	Strong analytical, leadership, and organizational skills
	Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities
	High level of integrity and attention to detail


Additional Information:


	Opportunity to work with a globally recognized hospitality brand under Accor
	Professional development and career growth opportunities within the Accor global network
	A collaborative and inclusive workplace culture
	Competitive compensation and employee benefits
	The opportunity to contribute to a dynamic hospitality environment delivering exceptional guest experiences.&nbsp;

Finance Manager Job at Accor Hotels




        
    

        
        Accor Hotel is a French multinational hospitality company that owns, manages, and franchises hotels, resorts, and vacation properties. It is the largest hospitality company in Europe and the sixth-largest hospitality company worldwide.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Finance Manager
Job Location: Lagos

Description
     

            
    




	We are looking for a dynamic and experienced Finance Manager to lead the financial operations of the hotel and support strategic decision-making that drives business performance and profitability.
	Reporting to the General Manager, the Finance Manager will oversee financial reporting, budgeting, compliance, and internal controls while ensuring alignment with the financial standards of Accor.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the overall financial operations of the hotel including accounting, reporting, and financial controls
	Prepare and analyze monthly financial reports, forecasts, and annual budgets
	Ensure compliance with Accor financial policies, internal audit standards, and statutory regulations
	Monitor cash flow, cost control, and revenue performance to support operational profitability
	Supervise and develop the finance team while ensuring adherence to financial procedures
	Coordinate internal and external audits and ensure timely resolution of audit findings
	Provide financial insights and strategic recommendations to support management decision-making
	Ensure timely tax filings, statutory payments, and regulatory compliance


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field
	5–10 years of relevant work experience, preferably in hospitality, service, or corporate environments
	ICAN, ACCA, or an internationally recognized equivalent professional certification
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting, budgeting, cost control, and internal audit processes
	Proficiency in accounting systems and financial management tools
	Strong analytical, leadership, and organizational skills
	Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities
	High level of integrity and attention to detail


Additional Information:


	Opportunity to work with a globally recognized hospitality brand under Accor
	Professional development and career growth opportunities within the Accor global network
	A collaborative and inclusive workplace culture
	Competitive compensation and employee benefits
	The opportunity to contribute to a dynamic hospitality environment delivering exceptional guest experiences. 
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of Sales",
  "description": "Golden Spoon Food Limited is a Nigerian food production and distribution company headquartered in the Kuje area of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT). They operate a nationwide distribution network for their food products. The company frequently expands its workforce in the Abuja region, hiring for full-time roles.&nbsp;

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Head of Sales

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements


	Applicants must possess at least a B.Sc. Business administration, Economics and other related fields
	2&ndash;4 years of experience as a Head of Sales or Leadership roles
	Applicant must live in Kuje, Abuja or Kuje Axis
	Applicants must possess strong background in Marketing, Sales or Customer services
	Applicants must be able to have good management skills
	Applicants must possess good analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Applicants must have good communication and teamwork skills.
	Applicants must be detail-oriented, trustworthy, and able to&nbsp;&nbsp;work with minimal supervision.


Salary
N120,000 - N150,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application and CV to: hr@goldenspoon.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: For more enquiries, call/text 08165982379.",

Head of Sales Job at Golden Spoon Food Limited




        
    

        
        Golden Spoon Food Limited is a Nigerian food production and distribution company headquartered in the Kuje area of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT). They operate a nationwide distribution network for their food products. The company frequently expands its workforce in the Abuja region, hiring for full-time roles. 

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Head of Sales

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements
     

            
    




	Applicants must possess at least a B.Sc. Business administration, Economics and other related fields
	2–4 years of experience as a Head of Sales or Leadership roles
	Applicant must live in Kuje, Abuja or Kuje Axis
	Applicants must possess strong background in Marketing, Sales or Customer services
	Applicants must be able to have good management skills
	Applicants must possess good analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Applicants must have good communication and teamwork skills.
	Applicants must be detail-oriented, trustworthy, and able to  work with minimal supervision.


Salary
N120,000 - N150,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application and CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: For more enquiries, call/text 08165982379.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Technical Recruiter",
  "description": "HR Aid is a startup consulting company that helps businesses with recruitment and HR needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Technical Recruiter

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time, On-site

Overview


	The Tech Recruiter, also known as a Technical Recruiter, is a specialized HR professional focused on attracting and hiring skilled candidates for technology-related roles such as software developers, engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity specialists, and IT support staff.


Responsibilities


	Talent Sourcing: Identify potential candidates through job boards, LinkedIn, niche platforms, networking events, and job fairs
	Screening and Evaluation: Review resumes, assess technical skills, conduct initial interviews, and coordinate technical assessments to ensure candidates meet role requirements
	Collaboration: Work with hiring managers to understand job requirements, technical skills needed, and team culture
	Candidate Engagement: Provide feedback, maintain relationships with past applicants, and promote the company&rsquo;s employer brand
	Recruitment Process Management: Manage the full recruitment cycle, including job postings, interview scheduling, offer negotiation, and onboarding coordination
	Industry Awareness: tay updated on technology trends, salary benchmarks, and hiring practices to attract top talent.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor`s / Master`s Degree with 4 - 5 years experience.
	Technical Knowledge: Understanding of technical roles, programming languages, software development processes, and IT infrastructure
	Communication Skills: Ability to clearly convey job requirements, candidate qualifications, and company culture to both candidates and hiring teams.
	Interpersonal Skills: Build strong relationships with candidates, hiring managers, and industry professionals
	Organizational Skills: Manage multiple open positions, track candidate pipelines, and maintain recruitment records efficiently
	Analytical Skills: Evaluate candidate skills, experience, and cultural fit to make informed hiring decisions.


Salary
Negotiable.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: talent@hr-aidconsults.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Technical Recruiter Job at HR Aid Consults




        
    

        
        HR Aid is a startup consulting company that helps businesses with recruitment and HR needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Technical Recruiter

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time, On-site

Overview


	The Tech Recruiter, also known as a Technical Recruiter, is a specialized HR professional focused on attracting and hiring skilled candidates for technology-related roles such as software developers, engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity specialists, and IT support staff.


Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Talent Sourcing: Identify potential candidates through job boards, LinkedIn, niche platforms, networking events, and job fairs
	Screening and Evaluation: Review resumes, assess technical skills, conduct initial interviews, and coordinate technical assessments to ensure candidates meet role requirements
	Collaboration: Work with hiring managers to understand job requirements, technical skills needed, and team culture
	Candidate Engagement: Provide feedback, maintain relationships with past applicants, and promote the company’s employer brand
	Recruitment Process Management: Manage the full recruitment cycle, including job postings, interview scheduling, offer negotiation, and onboarding coordination
	Industry Awareness: tay updated on technology trends, salary benchmarks, and hiring practices to attract top talent.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor`s / Master`s Degree with 4 - 5 years experience.
	Technical Knowledge: Understanding of technical roles, programming languages, software development processes, and IT infrastructure
	Communication Skills: Ability to clearly convey job requirements, candidate qualifications, and company culture to both candidates and hiring teams.
	Interpersonal Skills: Build strong relationships with candidates, hiring managers, and industry professionals
	Organizational Skills: Manage multiple open positions, track candidate pipelines, and maintain recruitment records efficiently
	Analytical Skills: Evaluate candidate skills, experience, and cultural fit to make informed hiring decisions.


Salary
Negotiable.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
